The Cost of Doing Nothing

Every unconfirmed appointment is money you've already spent and might never collect.

You paid for the marketing that brought that patient in. You held the slot. Your practitioner cleared their schedule. Then the patient just — doesn't show. No call, no warning. That slot is gone, and so is everything it cost you to fill it.

10–20%
of booked appointments at aesthetics clinics simply never show up — without an active confirmation system
$200–800
gone per missed slot — the treatment, the marketing spend behind it, and the idle practitioner time
Recovered
what this system gets back — slots filled and patients confirmed automatically, before they ever go quiet

Proof, Not Promises

Watch the system run, end to end

This is a real med spa's full booking and patient-communication system. No slides, no mockups. Real screen recordings of the workflows actually running.

✓ Real workflows ✓ Real patient messaging ✓ Built and tested in n8n
01
Booking

From booking form to EMR, with zero retyping

A patient submits a booking form. Their record is validated, encrypted, saved, and created directly inside the clinic's EMR — then they receive a branded confirmation on their preferred channel.

Before

Front desk re-types every booking into the EMR by hand.

After

Patient submits once. EMR and messaging update instantly.

Watch · Booking Workflow
Form
Workflow
Appointment record
02
Reminders & No-Shows

The system knows who hasn't confirmed — and follows up

72 hours out, every unconfirmed patient gets asked to confirm. If they don't respond by 24 hours out, they're asked again — automatically. Staff only step in when someone's truly unreachable.

Before

Staff manually call patients the day before, hoping they answer.

After

Patients self-confirm by reply. Staff see only who's still pending.

Watch · Reminder Workflow
72h SMS
24h SMS
2h SMS
03
Patient Replies

YES, CANCEL, or RESCHEDULE — handled the moment it's sent

A single text reply updates the patient's appointment status in real time, sends a branded acknowledgement, and for reschedules, hands them a self-serve link instead of a callback queue.

Before

Replies sit unread until someone checks the inbox.

After

Status updates in seconds. Patient gets an instant response.

Watch · Response Handler
YES reply
Status update
Reschedule flow
More patient-journey automations — follow-ups, review requests, reactivation campaigns — are being added as they're built.

What This Actually Does

Time back for your team. Revenue back for the business.

Every unconfirmed appointment is a slot that might sit empty. Every manual follow-up that doesn't happen is a patient who quietly stops coming back. This system is built to close both gaps — not just save staff hours.

Live in the system you can watch below

01

Booking & EMR Sync

Patient booking forms that feed straight into your EMR (like Cliniko) — no double entry, no double bookings, no front desk copying details by hand.

02

72h / 24h Reminders

Status-aware reminder sequences that know who's confirmed and who's gone quiet — and escalate automatically instead of treating everyone the same.

03

Patient Replies

A single text reply — YES, CANCEL, or RESCHEDULE — updates the patient's record and triggers the right next step, in real time.

04

No-Show Prevention

Your team finds out exactly who's still unconfirmed close to their appointment — recovering revenue that would otherwise just go quiet.

Why This, Not a Generic Reminder Tool

Built for clinics, not just calendars

Most "appointment automation" is a timer that sends a text. This tracks what actually happened, and reacts to it.

Status-aware, not just time-based

The system always knows whether a patient confirmed, cancelled, or went silent — and reacts differently to each, instead of blasting the same message to everyone.

Built on what you already run

No rip-and-replace, no new system for your team to learn. This works alongside your existing EMR and front desk process, not instead of it.

Tested against real edge cases, not demoed once

Phone number formats, duplicate replies, partial data, timing windows — the messy reality that breaks most automations was worked through before this ever reached a real patient.

Who's Building This

Hi, I'm Armstrong Eze.

I design and build the booking, reminder, and patient-communication systems med spas run on — so the front desk stops chasing confirmations by hand.

I have built and tested complete med spa booking, confirmation, reminder, and no-show prevention systems using self-hosted automation infrastructure. I care more about whether a workflow survives contact with a messy real-world edge case than whether a demo looks good for five minutes — every system on this page has been tested, broken, fixed, and tested again before I'd put my name on it.

If your clinic is still confirming appointments by phone call, or your front desk is re-typing bookings into your EMR by hand — that's usually where we start.

LinkedIn

Before You Ask

Common questions

—Will this work with the EMR we already use?

In most cases, yes. I build around what you currently use rather than asking you to switch systems — the goal is one less thing for your team to learn, not one more.

—Is our patient data safe?

Patient data is encrypted at rest, and every action that touches a record is logged. The system is built with HIPAA-conscious handling from day one, not added after the fact.

—How long does a build like this take?

It depends on scope, but most single-workflow builds (booking, or reminders, or response handling) take one to two weeks from our first call to a live, tested system.

—What happens if something breaks after launch?

I monitor what I build. Ongoing support is part of the offer, not a separate emergency call — if a workflow misfires, I fix it.

—Does this replace our front desk staff?

No — it removes the repetitive parts of their job (re-typing bookings, chasing confirmations by phone) so they can spend their time actually helping patients in front of them.
